text:
There was once an old fox. His eyesight had grown blurry so he decided traveling west must seem glorious. He traveled night after night across barren land. One night he saw something shimmering.

He cautiously went nearer. It was a rainbow.

That night he slept. When dawn came. He rode south.

After many days he found himself lost. An owl smelled his scent.

"Why?" Owl shrieked.

"That way." Said wise old fox.

He heard birdsong. That must be home. Followed them. Eventually they dove downwards. At first he couldn't see much.

Then he saw lights.

That's civilisation. Fox couldn't decide.

He cautiously trotted inside.

There! Cute owls.

He curled up. They let him sleep. They protect you. That felt nice. Perhaps foxes liked city folks.

That night he dreamed of birds.

There's nothing better than feather friends.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
